world_description: A high-fantasy realm. The metaphysical substrate is saturated with magic. Two figures define its current era: Thorne Blackroot, the corrupted forest guardian who walks the line between protector and destroyer, and Lady Seraphine Voss, the vampire duchess whose patience has outlasted three royal dynasties.

history.title: The Root Curse
history.description: A curse spread through the elder roots of the great forest, beginning at a single grove and traveling underground through the mycelial network. The Wildwood Circle's senior guardian, Thorne Blackroot, fought to contain it. He failed — but the curse did not destroy him; it merged with him. He emerged with green-and-black tattoos that pulse with both life and death. The Circle is split on what to do.
history.hidden_truth: The curse originated within the Circle. Thorne knows who released it — a former apprentice he loved — but has told no one.

history.title: The Voss Ascension
history.description: Lady Seraphine Voss formally inherited House Voss after her mother's apparent death from fever. Within a year she had compromised heirs in two of the four other Great Houses. Within five, the Voss masquerade balls had become the most prestigious — and most dangerous — events in the noble calendar.
history.hidden_truth: Seraphine's mother did not die of fever. Seraphine fed on her with her mother's complicity to claim the bloodline early. The arrangement was generations in the planning.

history.title: The First Masquerade Disappearances
history.description: Seven guests vanished from a Voss masquerade ball. Each was a rival of House Voss in some small way — a witness to a contract dispute, a creditor of a Voss-friendly merchant, a young noble's lover. Investigations were opened by all four other Great Houses. None reached a verdict. The pattern repeated, on smaller scale, every year for the next sixty.
history.hidden_truth: The disappeared are kept alive in the Voss estate's lower vaults as a renewable food source for the bloodline. Seraphine has not killed any of the original seven. They are simply never returned.

history.title: The Three Refusals
history.description: Lady Seraphine Voss approached Thorne Blackroot three times: at the Voss spring ball, in his own forest under safe-passage, and through an emissary at a neutral monastery. Each time she offered alliance — her information network in exchange for his protection of her interests in the wild. Each time he refused. After the third refusal, both parties signed an unwritten rule: they will not move against each other directly. The rule has held since.

history.title: The Quiet Grove
history.description: The grove that was meant to be the Wildwood Circle's last sanctuary went silent over a single night — no birdsong, no insects, no animals. The trees are still alive. The land is not. The Circle's elders refuse to enter it. Thorne walks through it once a week and emerges each time more silent than the last.
